Fetch peer profile via NATS credentials from invitation QR code

When scanning a connection invitation QR code, the app now uses the
embedded NATS credentials to connect to the inviter's message space
and read their published profile from JetStream, rather than relying
on profile data embedded in the QR code itself.

Changes:
- ConnectionsClient: Add fetchPeerProfile() with temporary NATS connection
  and JetStream consumer (deliver_policy: last) for retained profile
- ConnectionsClient: Add parseCredsFile() for NATS .creds format parsing
- CreateInvitationViewModel: Include nats_endpoint in QR code data
- ScanInvitationViewModel: Fetch peer profile after QR scan, populate
  Preview state with real name/email from vault

commit.message:
Classify ExternConstructorPrototype → §8.24 [skip ci]

Section 8.24 "Out-of-block declarations" governs the use of the extern qualifier on an in-class method prototype, including extern function new(...) (the class_constructor_prototype production). It defines the two-step mechanism: declare the prototype with extern inside the class body, then provide the implementation outside. There is no deeper subclause for the constructor-specific case.

commit.message:
fix(setup): wizard test connection and URL hints; security hardening (v2.0.7)

Setup wizard:
- Fix "No URL configured" on Test Connection — backend now accepts
  service-specific field names (e.g. sonarr_url) sent by hx-include,
  not just a generic "url" field
- Add URL format hint callouts on all wizard steps with URL fields,
  explaining Docker container names, host.docker.internal, and LAN IPs
- Remove duplicate hx-include and redundant JS workaround on Ollama button

Security (STRIDE + PASTA review):
- Enforce 8-character minimum password server-side in create_user and
  change_password (was frontend-only)
- Add COOKIE_SECURE env var (default true) so HTTP-only deployments can
  set secure=false instead of silently breaking session auth
- Prune transcoding job store: evict finished jobs after 24h, hard cap
  at 100 entries to prevent unbounded memory growth
- Log startup warning when TRANSCODE_ALLOWED_ROOTS is unset with Sonarr/Radarr
- Document COOKIE_SECURE and TRUSTED_PROXY in .env.example

commit.message:
Add vault-mediated NATS credential generation for connection invitations

Vault can now generate scoped NATS credentials when creating connection
invitations. The invitation holder can use these credentials to connect
to the inviter's NATS message space and read their published profile
from JetStream.

Changes:
- Vault-manager: Generate scoped NATS user JWTs signed with account seed
- Vault-manager: NATSProxy caches account seed, SealerProxy loads it
- Vault-manager: Profile publish subject changed to match ENROLLMENT stream
  (OwnerSpace.{ownerSpace}.forApp.profile.public)
- Supervisor: SealerHandler routes account seed requests to parent via vsock
- Parent: DynamoDB client fetches KMS-encrypted account seeds
- Parent: Handle nats_account_seed_get intermediate messages in vsock loop

commit.message:
fix: use string matcher format for Claude Code hooks (#18)

* fix: use string matcher format for Claude Code hooks

Claude Code expects the `matcher` field in hooks to be a regex string
(e.g., "Bash", "Edit|Write"), not an object like `{ tools: ["Bash"] }`.
The old object format causes a Settings Error on startup after running
`sniper init`.

Updated all hook definitions and the scaffolder merge logic to use the
correct string-based matcher format per the Claude Code hooks spec.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: auto-generate changeset for PR #18

* style: remove extra blank line in scaffolder

Harden workflow: pin actions to SHA, fix script injection, add dependabot

- Pin all actions (checkout, setup-python, setup-node, upload-artifact)
  to SHA hashes instead of mutable version tags
- Fix script injection vulnerabilities by moving commit-message-prefix,
  commit-paths, and command inputs to environment variables
- Replace `|| true` on command step with `continue-on-error: true` so
  the job status properly reflects failures
- Remove `|| true` from git pull --rebase and `|| echo` from git push
  so failures are visible
- Add .github/dependabot.yml for automated action version updates
- Add MIT LICENSE

commit.message:
Add Copilot code review instructions (#7)

* Add Copilot code review instructions

Project-specific guidance for Copilot code review covering Go
conventions, testing, database/migrations, security, and architecture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add workflow to gate PRs on Copilot code review

Triggered by pull_request_review events, checks that Copilot has
submitted a review before passing. Can be added as a required status
check to enforce Copilot review on all PRs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix MCP tool handler return type: pointer not value

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Note to re-request Copilot review when pushing to existing PRs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Copilot review gate: validate HEAD SHA, handle pagination

- Also trigger on pull_request (opened/synchronize/ready_for_review)
  so the check re-runs when new commits are pushed
- Validate that Copilot's review matches the current HEAD SHA
- Use --paginate to handle PRs with many reviews

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Poll for Copilot review instead of failing immediately

Waits up to 15 minutes (30 attempts × 30s) for Copilot to submit
a review on the current HEAD before failing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix paginated review check, skip draft PRs

- Use --paginate --slurp with flatten to get a single aggregated count
  across all pages instead of one count per page
- Skip draft PRs since Copilot doesn't review drafts
- Keep 'opened' trigger since Copilot auto-reviews on push (review_on_push
  is enabled in the branch ruleset)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix gh api: --slurp incompatible with --jq

Use per_page=100 instead of --paginate --slurp, since --slurp
cannot be combined with --jq in gh cli.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Stripe connection error on Vercel serverless

Create a fresh Stripe instance per request instead of caching
a singleton. Cached instances can hold stale TCP connections
between serverless invocations, causing "connection to Stripe"
retry errors.

- Admin endpoint: PUT /admin/users/{userId}/tier for manual activation
- Feature gating: notifications/reports split into free/gold routes
- Payment routes gated behind platinum tier
- Cron notifications only for gold+ managers
- Resident portal inherits manager's tier for feature access
- Free tier: WA link generation (click-to-open, no API send)
- Frontend: TierGate component with blur overlay + upgrade CTA
- Frontend: TierBadge in sidebar, pricing page with 3 tiers
- Frontend: tier-aware UI (CSV export, send buttons, expense breakdown)
- Auth context updated with tier + tier_limits from /auth/me

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add markdown reporters and refactor threshold logic (#9)

* Add comprehensive codebase review covering security, best practices, and improvements

Reviews 215+ TypeScript source files across security concerns (4 medium-severity
findings including symlink traversal and shell injection), Node.js best practices
(resource leaks, error handling, TypeScript safety), and 16 functionality improvement
opportunities (test coverage gaps, reporter format inconsistency, missing config file).
Includes prioritized roadmap across 3 phases.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByRTAoGS6Fwjs657efVnNQ

* fix: deduplicate threshold logic and add missing markdown reporters

Extract duplicated severity/level threshold comparison from audit, policy,
and lint commands into shared/threshold.ts. Add markdown reporters for lint,
policy, and verify commands (previously only supported terminal and JSON).
Centralize format-switch + file-write-or-stdout pattern into shared/reporter.ts
used by audit, lint, policy, and verify commands.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByRTAoGS6Fwjs657efVnNQ

* chore: update package-lock.json

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByRTAoGS6Fwjs657efVnNQ

* test: add tests for check, report, init, and refresh commands

47 tests covering the 4 previously untested command files:
- check (14 tests): JSON/terminal output, --ci exit codes, --product
  filter, version coercion warnings, fetch error handling
- report (10 tests): markdown/JSON output, stale/current sections,
  changelog links, summary counts, error resilience
- init (9 tests): auto-detection of known packages, merge of same-
  package skills, custom output path, schema correctness
- refresh (14 tests): --yes/--dry-run modes, LLM call verification,
  skills directory resolution, platform-versioned skip, product-
  version patching when LLM omits bump, --product filtering

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByRTAoGS6Fwjs657efVnNQ

* fix: resolve typecheck errors in command test files

- Add missing `path` property to SkillFile mock objects
- Use `Awaited<ReturnType<typeof resolveModel>>` for model mock
- Add explicit `Map<string, string | Error>` type annotations for
  mixed-type Map constructors in check, report, and refresh tests

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByRTAoGS6Fwjs657efVnNQ

* fix: address critical security and reliability issues

1. Replace stat() with lstat() across discovery, scanner, test runner,
   and grader files to prevent symlink-following attacks
2. Shell-escape user command in OCI isolation provider to prevent
   command injection via options.command in sh -c
3. Add AbortController with 15s timeout to npm registry fetch calls
   (fetchPackageMetadata and fetchLatestVersion)
4. Fix clearTimeout leak in URL checker by using try/finally around
   the fetch call so the timer is always cleared on error

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByRTAoGS6Fwjs657efVnNQ

* feat: phase 2 quality and UX improvements

1. Add 30s timeout to all git operations in verify/git.ts to prevent
   hanging on slow networks
2. Fix resource leak in refresh command's promptUser() — readline
   interface now handles error and premature close events
3. Standardize --quiet/--verbose flags across all commands:
   - Add --quiet to check, budget, lint, policy check, test
   - Add --verbose to budget, lint, policy check
   - Add mutual exclusivity validation for --quiet + --verbose
   - Wire --quiet through to formatAndOutput() and output paths

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByRTAoGS6Fwjs657efVnNQ

* feat: phase 3 extensibility improvements

- Add SARIF 2.1.0 reporters for lint, policy, verify, budget, and test
  commands, enabling GitHub Security tab integration across all features
- Add .skillscheckrc.yml config support with 3-level cascade
  (CLI flags > project config > user config)
- Add progress indicator infrastructure for verbose mode
- Export programmatic API via skills-check/api entry point with types
- Add composite `health` command running audit+lint+budget+policy
- Expand action.yml with per-command structured outputs
  (finding counts, token totals, pass/fail counts)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByRTAoGS6Fwjs657efVnNQ

commit.message:
Classify ConstructorDefaultBeforeArgs → §8.17 [skip ci]

§8.17 (Chaining constructors) defines the `default` keyword in a subclass constructor argument list. The LRM example on page 193 shows exactly `function new(default, bit enable)` — `default` appearing first, followed by additional arguments — which is precisely what the test exercises.

commit.message:
feat: Discord slash commands, threaded conversations, session resume, and GitHub mention ack (#736)

* feat: Discord slash commands, agent routing, council launching, and bot presence

- Register Discord Application Commands (slash commands) via PUT API on startup
  when DISCORD_APP_ID is configured; supports guild-scoped or global registration
- Handle INTERACTION_CREATE gateway events for slash command responses
- Add /agents, /switch, /council, /status, /new, /help commands (both text and slash)
- Add @AgentName mention routing to direct messages to specific agents
- Add per-user agent preference with /switch persistence
- Add bot presence/status via IDENTIFY payload and PRESENCE_UPDATE opcode
- Add corvid_launch_council MCP tool so agents can launch councils from any session
- Wire processManager through McpServiceContainer for council launching
- Add ContentBlock extraction for proper assistant message rendering
- Add prompt injection scanning and audit logging for Discord messages
- Update discord bridge spec to v2 with all new features documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Discord thread-based conversations and rich embed responses

- Each new conversation creates a Discord thread from the user's message,
  keeping the main channel clean and organized
- Any user can reply in a thread to join the conversation (shared sessions)
- Agent responses use rich embeds with agent name, model in footer,
  and a consistent per-agent color
- Remove @AgentName text routing (conflicts with Discord's @ mention system)
- Thread auto-archives after 24 hours of inactivity
- Update Discord bridge spec and tests for thread-based flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: populate /switch slash command with agent choices dropdown

Agent names and models are loaded from the database and registered as
Discord command choices, giving users a dropdown picker instead of
free-text input. Capped at 25 choices (Discord limit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update Discord bridge spec to v3

Reflects thread-based conversations, shared thread sessions, rich embed
responses, agent dropdown in /switch, and removal of @AgentName routing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI failures — SSRF validation and Windows test timing

- Add snowflake/token validation before Discord API URL interpolation
  to fix CodeQL SSRF alerts
- Increase CircuitBreaker HALF_OPEN timeout margin (60ms → 150ms)
  to fix flaky Windows CI failure
- Update test fixtures to use valid Discord snowflake IDs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: auto-acknowledge GitHub mentions and show Discord tool status

1. Polling: immediately post a GitHub comment when picking up a mention
   ("AgentName is looking into this.") and subscribe to session lifecycle
   events to guarantee a follow-up comment on completion, error, or stop.
   This ensures we never make a promise we can't keep.

2. Discord: show tool_status events in thread embeds so users can see
   what the agent is doing (e.g. "Creating work task...", "Searching...").
   Debounced at 3s to avoid flooding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI failures — SSRF validation and missing mock method

- Add encodeURIComponent to createThread URL params to satisfy CodeQL SSRF
  taint analysis (assertSnowflake already validates, but CodeQL needs
  explicit sanitization)
- Add subscribe/unsubscribe to mock processManager in polling tests to
  match new subscribeForCompletion feature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: auto-resume idle sessions on new messages

When a WebSocket send_message targets a session that isn't running,
look it up in the DB and resume it with conversation context instead
of returning an error. Discord bridge resumes stopped/error thread
sessions and recovers thread-to-session mappings from DB after
server restart. Session names now encode threadId for persistence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip redundant completion comment on normal session exit

The agent posts a substantive reply itself on success, so the generic
"finished working on this" comment is noise. Only post follow-up
comments on error or manual stop, where the agent didn't get to respond.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace Zavu SDK with Resend for email delivery

Switches the email provider from @zavudev/sdk to resend, updating
the cron send route to use resend.emails.send() with HTML support.

fix: remove gallery modal fade animation to prevent image overlap on rapid navigation

Eliminated 150ms setTimeout and fade-out CSS from updateModalImage() so rapid
button taps or swipes no longer stack multiple deferred callbacks causing ghost images.
